whats_new.title: Jakarta EE 8 full platform support
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 provides Jakarta EE 8 full platform support.

whats_new.title: Java SE 17 and 21 certification
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 is certified for Java SE 17 and Java SE 21.

whats_new.title: WebLogic Migration Analysis Tool
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds a command line migration analysis utility that identifies removed or unused APIs in WebLogic applications.

whats_new.title: OpenRewrite recipes
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 provides OpenRewrite recipes to simplify migrating WebLogic applications to a new WebLogic and Java version.

whats_new.title: Secured production mode enabled by default
whats_new.description: When production mode is selected in WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0, secured production mode defaults are automatically enabled.

whats_new.title: OpenID Connect authentication provider
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds a WebLogic OpenID Connect provider for authentication and identity assertion.

whats_new.title: Cluster-targeted JMS automatic rebalance
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds an option to automatically rebalance running cluster-targeted JMS instances when the system is idle.

behavior_changes.title: 14c JDK interoperability limitations
behavior_changes.description: Oracle documents interoperability limitations for WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 instances and Java IIOP clients running on JDK 17 or 21.

behavior_changes.title: Production mode enables stricter security defaults
behavior_changes.description: Selecting production mode automatically enables secured production mode, changing default security behavior for new domains.

behavior_changes.title: Demo certificate authority is per-domain
behavior_changes.description: The demonstration certificate authority is unique to each new domain and uses PKCS12 identity and trust keystores.

deprecated.title: Legacy API migration analysis required
deprecated.description: Oracle recommends using migration analysis tooling to identify APIs removed or no longer used in WebLogic Server 14c.
